# heat-treatment

React Native Expo app for heat treatment operations.

Core routes:
- `app/(auth)/login.tsx`
- `app/(main)/(tabs)/index.tsx`
- `app/(main)/(tabs)/batches.tsx`
- `app/(main)/(tabs)/devices.tsx`
- `app/(main)/(tabs)/alerts.tsx`
- `app/(main)/batches/create.tsx`
- `app/(main)/batches/[id].tsx`
- `app/(main)/devices/create.tsx`
- `app/(main)/devices/[id].tsx`
- `app/(main)/alerts/[id].tsx`
- `app/(main)/account.tsx`
- `app/(main)/settings.tsx`
- `app/(main)/alert-settings.tsx`

Tech stack:
- React Native 0.83.6 + Expo SDK 55
- TypeScript 5.9 strict
- expo-router
- react-native-unistyles
- @tanstack/react-query
- Zustand
- Axios
- react-i18next with EN / VI / zh-CN / zh-TW
- react-native-mmkv
- react-hook-form + zod/v4

Important conventions:
- Use `useTranslation()` for visible text.
- Use `StyleSheet.create((theme) => ...)` from `react-native-unistyles`.
- Use `@/services/api` for HTTP calls.
- Use `useAuthStore` for auth state.
- Use `yarn validate` before merging.
- Use `yarn sync:version` when release version changes.

Versioning:
- `app.config.ts` is the source of truth.
- `package.json` mirrors app version.
- `src/lib/constants/app-version.ts` exposes runtime version constants.
